APPEARANCES: James P. Nevin, Esq. of Brayton Purcell, LLP 898-1555 for pltf & opposing party; Troy D. McMahan, Esq. of Filice Brown Eassa & McLeod LLP 510/287-8185 for deft & moving party.
RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling - GRANTED. DEFENDANT SHIFTED THE BURDEN OF PRODUCING EVIDENCE BASED ON PLAINTIFF'S FACTUALLY DEVOID SPECIAL INTERROGATORY RESPONSES AND PLAINTIFF'S DEPOSITION TESTIMONY. PLAINTIFF'S OBJECTIONS TO AYDELOTTE DECLARATION ARE SUSTAINED FOR LACK OF FOUNDATIONAL FACTS. PLAINTIFF FAILS TO PRESENT EVIDENCE THAT CREATES A TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT. DEFENDANT'S OJBECTIONS 1, 3, 7-9 ARE SUSTAINED. ONE IS BASED ON D'AMICO, THREE ON RELEVANCE AND 7-9 BASED ON RELIANCE ON INADMISSIBLE EVIDENCE. Order on motion signed in open court. Court ruled on both plaintiff's and defendant's evidentiary objections, both orders signed. (302/PJM/VC) Judge: Patrick J. Mahoney, Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR 8600
